tldr.title: Apple reveals new AI architecture built around Google Gemini models
tldr.author: unclefuzzy
bullets: Apple integrates Google Gemini alongside OpenAI GPT-4o to handle complex, off-device queries within its multi-tier Apple Intelligence architecture.
Apple's proprietary foundation models (on-device and cloud) were trained using Google's custom Cloud TPUv4 and TPUv5p hardware clusters.
Orchestration routing dynamically sends tasks to local hardware, Apple's Private Cloud Compute (PCC), or third-party APIs based on complexity.

tldr.title: What are you doing this week?
tldr.author: caius
bullets: Engineering community threads serve as informal peer reviews for systems programming, custom compilers, and self-hosted infrastructure projects.
Sharing active workflows helps developers benchmark toolchains, discover alternative libraries, and troubleshoot complex architectural bottlenecks.
Discussing side projects, from Rust utilities to Nix configurations, fosters collaborative knowledge exchange among senior technical practitioners.

tldr.title: 1k Data Breaches Later, the Disclosure Lag Is Worse
tldr.author: 882542F3884314B
bullets: Despite stricter regulations like the SEC four-day rule, the median time between breach detection and public disclosure continues to widen.
Protracted disclosure windows leave defense teams blind to active credential stuffing, phishing campaigns, and secondary supply-chain compromises.
Organizations must unify SIEM/XDR telemetry with legal incident response playbooks to accelerate compliance reporting and mitigate downstream exploitation.

tldr.title: Safe Terraform auto-apply with conftest
tldr.author: ricardbejarano
bullets: Export Terraform plans to JSON with `terraform show -json` to programmatically evaluate infrastructure changes before automated CI/CD execution.
Write policy-as-code guardrails in Rego using Conftest to automatically block high-risk changes, like open security groups or deleted databases.
Embed Conftest checks in GitLab CI or GitHub Actions to safely enable `terraform apply -auto-approve` on compliant pull requests.

tldr.title: We Think the SpaceX IPO Is Overvalued
tldr.author: 0xedb
bullets: Starlink's valuation hinges on global consumer broadband TAM, which faces limits from urban fiber expansion and high user terminal costs.
Starship development demands immense capital, while commoditizing orbital launch risks lowering profit margins despite Falcon 9's current dominance.
Skeptics argue SpaceX is valued like a high-multiple SaaS company rather than a capital-intensive aerospace hardware and infrastructure provider.
